label: Set Theory
shortDescription: The foundation of modern mathematics: sets, ordinals, cardinals, and independence.
modulesDescription: Read the set-theory chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Set Theory for Free | CalcPath
ogDescription: Master naive and axiomatic set theory, Zermelo-Fraenkel axioms, ordinals, cardinals, the axiom of choice and its equivalents, forcing, independence results, and advanced topics like large cardinals and Martin's axiom — with ELI5 explanations, worked examples, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete set theory course covering foundations (sets, elements, subsets, unions), the ZFC axioms, ordinals and cardinals, transfinite methods, the axiom of choice and equivalents (Zorn, well-ordering, Tychonoff), independence and forcing, and advanced topics in descriptive set theory and forcing axioms. Features ELI5 intuition, step-by-step worked examples, common mistakes, and practice problems for each lesson.

topics.title: Naive Set Theory
topics.description: Sets, elements, subsets, power sets, unions, and intersections — the language of modern mathematics.

topics.title: ZFC Axioms
topics.description: The Zermelo–Fraenkel axioms: extensionality, regularity, infinity, power set, and replacement.

topics.title: Ordinals & Cardinals
topics.description: Ordinal and cardinal numbers, alephs, the continuum hypothesis, and transfinite induction.

topics.title: Axiom of Choice & Equivalents
topics.description: The axiom of choice and its equivalents: Zorn's lemma, well-ordering theorems, and Tychonoff's theorem.

topics.title: Independence & Forcing
topics.description: Independence results, forcing, proper forcing, inner models, and Gödel's constructible universe.

topics.title: Advanced Set Theory
topics.description: Descriptive set theory, large cardinals, and Martin's axiom.
